hypothesis: A generative model trained to jointly predict both the crystal structure and its space group symmetry directly from XRD patterns—where symmetry classification is used as an auxiliary task to enforce symmetry-consistent latent representations—will produce candidate structures that, when subsequently relaxed with a machine learning interatomic potential, converge to the correct ground-truth phase at significantly higher rates than models conditioned on XRD alone, because the symmetry inductive bias eliminates the non-convex local minima along symmetry axes identified as the primary failure mode of gradient-descent-based XRD optimization.
